text.title: Value Stream Management Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure: instrument → map → measure DORA → identify constraint → experiment. Each step gates the next via observable invariants.

step.name: instrument
step.input: Jira/Linear, GitHub/GitLab, deploy log, PagerDuty, Zendesk; tool ID-link map
step.action: Wire connectors. Normalize timestamps to UTC. Audit a known-good item end-to-end and verify the chain.
step.decision-gate: If coverage < 3 sources OR < 90 days timestamp data, STOP — instrument first.

step.name: map
step.input: One representative work item per work type
step.action: Trace each item end-to-end across the wire. Identify stages. Validate `customer_anchor` is at user-request and value-received endpoints, not internal.
step.output: Stream map: stages + handoffs + work-type taxonomy
step.decision-gate: If `customer_anchor` cannot be anchored at customer-visible events, reject the map.

step.name: measure-dora
step.input: Last 90 days of git + deploy + incident logs
step.action: Run `dora-collector`. Split DF into `human_prs` + `bot_prs`. Compute CLT, CFR, MTTR. Reconcile webhook stream against daily full-pull.

step.name: identify-constraint
step.input: Stream map + 90-day flow data per work type
step.action: Run `bottleneck-analyzer`. Apply Little's Law per stage; identify highest `cycle_time * WIP`. Require sample_size >= 50 per work type.
step.decision-gate: If any work type has sample_size < 50, emit `insufficient_data: true` and stop.

step.name: experiment
step.action: Generate 1-3 ranked experiments with expected_lift + cost. Run highest-ranked experiment on the constraint; track before/after distribution over 2 sprints minimum.
step.decision-gate: After 2 sprints, re-run step 4 to confirm constraint moved; if not, re-rank experiments.
